summary: Added `make release-preflight` as the canonical Archive-readiness gate. The gate refuses dirty worktrees, unmirrored branches, and uncommitted Xcode build-number drift before running the existing build-provenance checks. `make release-check` now runs release-preflight before runtime validation, and release/validation/PR docs explain the three separate checkpoints: pre-push protects source history, release-preflight protects Archive readiness, and Build Info verifies the installed TestFlight binary.

contract_status_changes.before: Release work had build-provenance and pre-push checks, but no single command that required clean, mirrored, committed build-number source immediately before Xcode Archive.
contract_status_changes.after: `make release-preflight` is the explicit Archive gate. It checks clean tree, upstream mirror status, committed build-number provenance, and existing build-provenance output before Archive.

contract_status_changes.before: Release docs warned that hooks cannot stop Xcode Archive, but the required pre-Archive command was a set of separate shell commands.
contract_status_changes.after: Release, validation, and PR hygiene docs now name `make release-preflight` as the single pre-Archive command and distinguish it from pre-push and Build Info gates.

residual_risks: No hook or script can stop a manual Xcode Organizer Archive click; operators must run `make release-preflight` immediately before Archive.
This does not retry or claim TestFlight proof.
`make release-preflight` requires `HEAD...@{u}` to be `0 0`, so it is expected to pass only after this slice is committed and pushed.
recommended_next_reason: After a new TestFlight build is archived from clean committed source using `make release-preflight`, retry the Build Info gate before capturing Continue surfaces.
